# rag-llamaindex-qdrant-docker

> A beginner-friendly, step-by-step tutorial for building a multi-collection RAG with LlamaIndex, Qdrant, and Docker. Runnable by humans or AI agents. Pick OpenAI or Ollama embeddings.

This repository is the canonical source for the tutorial post "Build a RAG System with LlamaIndex and Qdrant in Docker", published on Medium (Towards Data Science) with mirrors on Hashnode and Hacker Noon.

## Core docs

- [README.md](README.md): Full tutorial — 6 steps from zero to a working RAG.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Condensed runbook for AI agents (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, etc.).
- [docs/architecture.png](docs/architecture.png): Stack diagram.
- [docs/troubleshooting.md](docs/troubleshooting.md): Top errors with fixes; supported file formats.

## Configuration

- [prerequisites.json](prerequisites.json): Machine-readable required tools/versions/env/resources.
- [collections.yaml.example](collections.yaml.example): Template for declaring collections.
- [.env.example](.env.example): Template with OpenAI and Ollama vars stacked.

## Optional

- Add Infisical for Secret Management — companion post (link added on publish).
- Add an MCP Server to Your RAG — companion post (link added on publish).

## Out of scope

- Secret management (deferred to companion post 1).
- MCP server / agent integration (deferred to companion post 2).
- Reranking, AutoMergingRetriever, SubQuestionQueryEngine, multi-modal — future improvements only.
- Authentication on Qdrant — local-only stack, secured by 127.0.0.1 binding.
- Windows-specific instructions — Linux/macOS only, with a one-line WSL note.
